== Hot Springs == * {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)}} – Located in Abira Town, Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido. * {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)}} – Located in Semboku City, Akita Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Tokyo)|ko|쓰루노유 온천 (도쿄도)|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (東京都)}} – Located in Okutama Town, Nishitama District, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (和歌山県)}} – Located in Minabe Town, Hidaka District, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Public Baths (Sento) == * {{ill|Tsuruno-yu (Yamagata)|ja|鶴乃湯 (山形県)}} – A former sento (public bath) in Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruno-yu Onsen}} [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Hot springs with identical names]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]]

[[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 鶴の湯温泉 |画像 = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |コメント = 鶴の湯温泉本陣（[[登録有形文化財]]）{{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]]田沢湖田沢字湯ノ岱2番地先先達沢国有林生保内事業区50林班8小班 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |交通アクセス = [[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の[[路線バス]]「乳頭温泉」行きで約40分、「鶴の湯温泉入口」下車。その後徒歩1.5km
車：[[東北自動車道]]・[[盛岡インターチェンジ|盛岡IC]]より[[国道46号]]・[[国道341号]]を通り、[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]と[[岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]で約60分 |泉質 = 含硫黄-ナトリウム・カルシウム-塩化物・炭酸水素泉など
''詳しくは[[#泉質|泉質]]の項を参照のこと'' |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |特記事項 = }} '''鶴の湯温泉'''（つるのゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]内にある[[温泉]]である。旅館「鶴の湯温泉」と、その別館である「山の宿」がある。 == 泉質 == 白湯、黒湯、中の湯、滝の湯の4種類の源泉が存在し、泉質はそれぞれ以下の通りである。 * 白湯:含硫黄-ナトリウム・カルシウム-塩化物・炭酸水素泉（硫化水素型） * 黒湯:ナトリウム-塩化物・炭酸水素塩泉 * 中の湯:含重曹・食塩硫化水素泉 * 滝の湯:含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物・炭酸水素泉 == 温泉街 == [[ファイル:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|鶴の湯温泉・山の宿]] 県道から外れて山道を走った先、ブナの原生林に囲まれたなかに、[[日本秘湯を守る会]]にも加盟している一軒宿の「鶴の湯温泉」がある。館主の佐藤和志は、同会の副会長を務めている。 建物は、茅葺屋根の[[本陣]]が有名で、他の建物も含めて全体が鄙びたイメージで統一されている。 白湯が足元から湧出してくる混浴の[[露天風呂]]は、鶴の湯のイメージを代表するものである。またこの露天風呂は乳頭温泉郷を紹介するときにイメージとして多く取り上げられ、さらには[[秘湯]]のイメージを代表するものとしても多く取り上げられている。 [[旅館]]の手前の[[林道]]沿いには、本館から引湯した別館「山の宿」があり、貸切露天風呂を備える。山の宿宿泊者は、本館の温泉も利用できる。日帰り入浴客が訪問する本館とは異なる静かな趣きの宿である。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|「鶴の湯温泉」敷地内の[[ミズバショウ]]]] 鶴の湯温泉は乳頭温泉郷でもっとも古くからある温泉場といわれている。発見は[[江戸時代]]とされる。開湯当初は「田沢の湯」と呼ばれた。[[秋田藩]]の[[佐竹義隆]]も[[湯治]]に訪れたことがあり、本陣という建物があるのはその名残である。ただしこの本陣には警護のものが泊まったとされる。この本陣は2010年に[[登録有形文化財]]に登録された。 [[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）に鶴が傷を癒している所を勘助という[[マタギ]]によって発見された。このとき、これに因んで温泉名も「田沢の湯」から「鶴の湯」となった。 一軒宿が開かれたのはその後であり、農閑期の[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]として営業を続けていた。 [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。 [[1980年代]]に入り、秘湯ブームと共に大きく脚光を浴びるようになった。 [[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）[[2月]]に、[[平成18年豪雪]]の影響で[[雪崩]]が発生し、巻き込まれた従業員1名が死亡し、客など16名が負傷した[[n:秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|ウィキニュース]]参照。そのため、冬季中、本陣は臨時休業し、別館のみの営業であったが、同年[[4月20日]]から営業を再開した。 [[2011年]]（平成23年）[[3月5日]]に放送された『[[嵐にしやがれ]]』（[[日本テレビ放送網|日本テレビ]]系列）では、ゲストが秋田県出身・在住の[[柳葉敏郎]]ということもあり、当温泉を再現したスタジオセットが作成され、温泉の湯を通常営業では行わない[[足湯]]として利用した。 == アクセス == * 公共交通：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の[[路線バス]]「乳頭温泉」行きで約40分、「鶴の湯温泉入口」下車。その後徒歩1.5km :: もしくは、同バス「アルパこまくさ」バス停下車。その後送迎（要予約）。 * 車：[[東北自動車道]]・[[盛岡インターチェンジ|盛岡IC]]より[[国道46号]]・[[国道341号]]を通り、[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]と[[岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]で約60分。車は旅館の前まで進入可。 == 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[黒湯温泉]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Wikinews|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ 鶴の湯温泉]（一軒宿のHP） * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 鶴の湯温泉]（ブログ） * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html 鶴の湯] - 乳頭温泉組合 {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるのゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:秋田県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]] [[Category:秋田佐竹家|湯つるのゆおんせん]]

車：[[東北自動車道]]・[[盛岡インターチェンジ|盛岡IC]]より[[国道46号]]・[[国道341号]]を通り、[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]と[[岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]で約60分 |泉質 = 含硫黄-ナトリウム・カルシウム-塩化物・炭酸水素泉など
''詳しくは[[#泉質|泉質]]の項を参照のこと'' |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |特記事項 = }} '''鶴の湯温泉'''（つるのゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]内にある[[温泉]]である。旅館「鶴の湯温泉」と、その別館である「山の宿」がある。 == 泉質 == 白湯、黒湯、中の湯、滝の湯の4種類の源泉が存在し、泉質はそれぞれ以下の通りである。 * 白湯:含硫黄-ナトリウム・カルシウム-塩化物・炭酸水素泉（硫化水素型） * 黒湯:ナトリウム-塩化物・炭酸水素塩泉 * 中の湯:含重曹・食塩硫化水素泉 * 滝の湯:含硫黄-ナトリウム-塩化物・炭酸水素泉 == 温泉街 == [[ファイル:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|鶴の湯温泉・山の宿]] 県道から外れて山道を走った先、ブナの原生林に囲まれたなかに、[[日本秘湯を守る会]]にも加盟している一軒宿の「鶴の湯温泉」がある。館主の佐藤和志は、同会の副会長を務めている。 建物は、茅葺屋根の[[本陣]]が有名で、他の建物も含めて全体が鄙びたイメージで統一されている。 白湯が足元から湧出してくる混浴の[[露天風呂]]は、鶴の湯のイメージを代表するものである。またこの露天風呂は乳頭温泉郷を紹介するときにイメージとして多く取り上げられ、さらには[[秘湯]]のイメージを代表するものとしても多く取り上げられている。 [[旅館]]の手前の[[林道]]沿いには、本館から引湯した別館「山の宿」があり、貸切露天風呂を備える。山の宿宿泊者は、本館の温泉も利用できる。日帰り入浴客が訪問する本館とは異なる静かな趣きの宿である。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|「鶴の湯温泉」敷地内の[[ミズバショウ]]]] 鶴の湯温泉は乳頭温泉郷でもっとも古くからある温泉場といわれている。発見は[[江戸時代]]とされる。開湯当初は「田沢の湯」と呼ばれた。[[秋田藩]]の[[佐竹義隆]]も[[湯治]]に訪れたことがあり、本陣という建物があるのはその名残である。ただしこの本陣には警護のものが泊まったとされる。この本陣は2010年に[[登録有形文化財]]に登録された。 [[1708年]]（[[宝永]]5年）に鶴が傷を癒している所を勘助という[[マタギ]]によって発見された。このとき、これに因んで温泉名も「田沢の湯」から「鶴の湯」となった。 一軒宿が開かれたのはその後であり、農閑期の[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]として営業を続けていた。 [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。 [[1980年代]]に入り、秘湯ブームと共に大きく脚光を浴びるようになった。 [[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）[[2月]]に、[[平成18年豪雪]]の影響で[[雪崩]]が発生し、巻き込まれた従業員1名が死亡し、客など16名が負傷した[[n:秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|ウィキニュース]]参照。そのため、冬季中、本陣は臨時休業し、別館のみの営業であったが、同年[[4月20日]]から営業を再開した。 [[2011年]]（平成23年）[[3月5日]]に放送された『[[嵐にしやがれ]]』（[[日本テレビ放送網|日本テレビ]]系列）では、ゲストが秋田県出身・在住の[[柳葉敏郎]]ということもあり、当温泉を再現したスタジオセットが作成され、温泉の湯を通常営業では行わない[[足湯]]として利用した。 == アクセス == * 公共交通：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の[[路線バス]]「乳頭温泉」行きで約40分、「鶴の湯温泉入口」下車。その後徒歩1.5km :: もしくは、同バス「アルパこまくさ」バス停下車。その後送迎（要予約）。 * 車：[[東北自動車道]]・[[盛岡インターチェンジ|盛岡IC]]より[[国道46号]]・[[国道341号]]を通り、[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]と[[岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]で約60分。車は旅館の前まで進入可。 == 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[黒湯温泉]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Wikinews|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ 鶴の湯温泉]（一軒宿のHP） * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 鶴の湯温泉]（ブログ） * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html 鶴の湯] - 乳頭温泉組合 {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるのゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:秋田県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]] [[Category:秋田佐竹家|湯つるのゆおんせん]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)}}

{{Japanese Hot Spring Area |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |Image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18年豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉]]

{{Japanese Hot Spring Area |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18年豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉]]

{{Japanese Hot Spring Area |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18年豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉]]

In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉]]

{{Japanese Hot Spring Area |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]]

{{Japanese Hot Spring Area |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox Onsen Area |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Hot Spring}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]
