This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=draft}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=draft}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=draft}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣|12=draft}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=draft}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=draft}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=draft}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣|12=draft}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=draft}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=draft}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=draft}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣|12=draft}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=draft}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=draft}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=draft}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣|12=draft}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=draft}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=draft}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=draft}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣|12=draft}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft|12=draft}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft|12=draft}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=draft|12=draft}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Hot Spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Semboku City|ja|仙北市|12=draft|12=draft}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is a {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|12=draft|12=draft}} named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=draft|12=draft}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Main Lodge|ja|本陣|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|12=draft|12=draft}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=draft|12=draft}}. Along the {{ill|Forest Road|ja|林道|12=draft|12=draft}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Mizubashō|ja|ミズバショウ|12=draft|12=draft}}]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka|ja|佐竹義隆|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|12=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei era|ja|宝永|12=draft|12=draft}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft|12=draft}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|avalanche|ja|雪崩|12=draft|12=draft}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=draft|12=draft}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=draft|12=draft}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=draft|12=draft}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television Network|ja|日本テレビ放送網|lt=Nippon TV|lt_ja=日本テレビ|12=draft|12=draft}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshio Yanagiba|ja|柳葉敏郎|12=draft|12=draft}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Foot Bath|ja|足湯|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅|12=draft|12=draft}} and then the {{ill|Local Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=draft|12=draft}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, via {{ill|National Route 46|ja|国道46号|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|National Route 341|ja|国道341号|12=draft|12=draft}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=draft|12=draft}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsenkyo|ja|乳頭温泉郷|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Semboku City]] [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]
