revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]][[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]][[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}}’s [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]] [[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] within [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. There is a [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|qq=}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof [[:en:Honjin|Main Lodge]], and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]], and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the [[:en:Forest track|Forest Road]] in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – [[:en:Lysichiton camtschatcensis|Mizubashō]]]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. Its discovery is attributed to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that [[:en:Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|Satake Yoshitaka]] of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]]), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|qq=}} as part of [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an [[:en:Avalanche|avalanche]] occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Satake Family of Akita]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:同名の温泉]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]][[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]][[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}}’s [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]] [[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] within [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. There is a [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|qq=}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof [[:en:Honjin|Main Lodge]], and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]], and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the [[:en:Forest track|Forest Road]] in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – [[:en:Lysichiton camtschatcensis|Mizubashō]]]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. Its discovery is attributed to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that [[:en:Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|Satake Yoshitaka]] of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]]), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|qq=}} as part of [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an [[:en:Avalanche|avalanche]] occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:同名の温泉]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Rotenburo" to "Outdoor hot spring baths"

Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:同名の温泉]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]][[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]][[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}}’s [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]] [[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] within [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. There is a [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|qq=}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof [[:en:Honjin|Main Lodge]], and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]], and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the [[:en:Forest track|Forest Road]] in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – [[:en:Lysichiton camtschatcensis|Mizubashō]]]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. Its discovery is attributed to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that [[:en:Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|Satake Yoshitaka]] of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]]), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|qq=}} as part of [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an [[:en:Avalanche|avalanche]] occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:Hot Springs with the Same Name]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]]

It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – [[:en:Lysichiton camtschatcensis|Mizubashō]]]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. Its discovery is attributed to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that [[:en:Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|Satake Yoshitaka]] of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]]), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|qq=}} as part of [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an [[:en:Avalanche|avalanche]] occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:Hot Springs with the Same Name]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11255725]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen |image = Tsurunoyu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsurunoyu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]][[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]][[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}}’s [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]] [[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] within [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. There is a [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|qq=}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof [[:en:Honjin|Main Lodge]], and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]], and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the [[:en:Forest track|Forest Road]] in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – [[:en:Lysichiton camtschatcensis|Mizubashō]]]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. Its discovery is attributed to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that [[:en:Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|Satake Yoshitaka]] of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]]), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治}} during the off‐season. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]] [[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku City]] within [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. There is a [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] named "Tsurunoyu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsurunoyu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|qq=}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof [[:en:Honjin|Main Lodge]], and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsurunoyu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]], and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the [[:en:Forest track|Forest Road]] in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsurunoyu Onsen" within the premises – [[:en:Lysichiton camtschatcensis|Mizubashō]]]] Tsurunoyu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. Its discovery is attributed to the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that [[:en:Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|Satake Yoshitaka]] of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|qq=draft|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the [[:en:Hōei|Hōei era]]), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|qq=}} as part of [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]]. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an [[:en:Avalanche|avalanche]] occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:Hot Springs with the Same Name]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:qq]]

In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an [[:en:Avalanche|avalanche]] occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on [[:en:April 20|April 20]] of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|qq=|1=Arashi ni Shiyagare|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on [[:en:Nippon Television|Nippon TV]]) featured guest [[:en:Toshirō Yanagiba|Toshio Yanagiba]], a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a [[:en:Ashiyu|Foot Bath]], which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] from [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]] and then the [[:en:Transit bus|Local Bus]] of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|qq=|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyuto Spa Resort|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|qq=|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via [[:en:Japan National Route 46|National Route 46]] and [[:en:Japan National Route 341|National Route 341]], then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|qq=|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|qq=|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyo]] ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|qq=|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|qq=|1=Myounoyu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsurunoyu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉|version=89030963|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:Hot Springs with the Same Name]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[Category:qq]]
