In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|Avalanche|en|Avalanche|WD=Q7935|lt=avalanche|1=Avalanche}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|1=Q11054479|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television|en|Nippon Television|WD=Q864910|lt=Nippon TV|1=Nippon Television}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshirō Yanagiba|en|Toshirō Yanagiba|WD=Q1135347|lt=Toshio Yanagiba|1=Toshirō Yanagiba}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|WD=Q725522|lt=Foot Bath|1=Ashiyu}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} and then the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|1=Q11446830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] {{wikidata link|Q11675866}} [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs with the Same Name]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsuruno-yu Onsen |image = Tsuruno-yu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsuruno-yu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}}’s {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}} {{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. There is a {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} named "Tsuruno-yu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsuruno-yu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Honjin|en|Honjin|WD=Q994817|lt=Main Lodge|1=Honjin}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsuruno-yu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the {{ill|Forest track|en|Forest track|WD=Q3444700|lt=Forest Road|1=Forest track}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsuruno-yu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|en|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|WD=Q772647|lt=Mizubashō|1=Lysichiton camtschatcensis}}]] Tsuruno-yu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|en|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|WD=Q8514544|lt=Satake Yoshitaka|1=Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩|1=Kubota Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067720}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|Avalanche|en|Avalanche|WD=Q7935|lt=avalanche|1=Avalanche}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|1=Q11054479|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television|en|Nippon Television|WD=Q864910|lt=Nippon TV|1=Nippon Television}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshirō Yanagiba|en|Toshirō Yanagiba|WD=Q1135347|lt=Toshio Yanagiba|1=Toshirō Yanagiba}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|WD=Q725522|lt=Foot Bath|1=Ashiyu}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} and then the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|1=Q11446830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] {{wikidata link|Q11675866}} [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Hot Springs with the Same Name]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsuruno-yu Onsen |image = Tsuruno-yu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsuruno-yu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}}’s {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}} {{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. There is a {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} named "Tsuruno-yu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsuruno-yu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Honjin|en|Honjin|WD=Q994817|lt=Main Lodge|1=Honjin}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsuruno-yu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the {{ill|Forest track|en|Forest track|WD=Q3444700|lt=Forest Road|1=Forest track}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsuruno-yu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|en|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|WD=Q772647|lt=Mizubashō|1=Lysichiton camtschatcensis}}]] Tsuruno-yu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|en|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|WD=Q8514544|lt=Satake Yoshitaka|1=Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩|1=Kubota Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067720}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|Avalanche|en|Avalanche|WD=Q7935|lt=avalanche|1=Avalanche}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|1=Q11054479|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television|en|Nippon Television|WD=Q864910|lt=Nippon TV|1=Nippon Television}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshirō Yanagiba|en|Toshirō Yanagiba|WD=Q1135347|lt=Toshio Yanagiba|1=Toshirō Yanagiba}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|WD=Q725522|lt=Foot Bath|1=Ashiyu}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} and then the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|1=Q11446830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] {{wikidata link|Q11675866}} [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}} {{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. There is a {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} named "Tsuruno-yu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsuruno-yu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Honjin|en|Honjin|WD=Q994817|lt=Main Lodge|1=Honjin}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsuruno-yu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the {{ill|Forest track|en|Forest track|WD=Q3444700|lt=Forest Road|1=Forest track}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsuruno-yu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|en|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|WD=Q772647|lt=Mizubashō|1=Lysichiton camtschatcensis}}]] Tsuruno-yu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|en|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|WD=Q8514544|lt=Satake Yoshitaka|1=Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩|1=Kubota Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067720}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|Avalanche|en|Avalanche|WD=Q7935|lt=avalanche|1=Avalanche}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|1=Q11054479|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television|en|Nippon Television|WD=Q864910|lt=Nippon TV|1=Nippon Television}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshirō Yanagiba|en|Toshirō Yanagiba|WD=Q1135347|lt=Toshio Yanagiba|1=Toshirō Yanagiba}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|WD=Q725522|lt=Foot Bath|1=Ashiyu}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} and then the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|1=Q11446830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] {{wikidata link|Q11675866}} [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsuruno-yu Onsen |image = Tsuruno-yu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsuruno-yu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}}’s {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}} {{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. There is a {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} named "Tsuruno-yu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsuruno-yu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Honjin|en|Honjin|WD=Q994817|lt=Main Lodge|1=Honjin}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsuruno-yu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the {{ill|Forest track|en|Forest track|WD=Q3444700|lt=Forest Road|1=Forest track}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsuruno-yu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|en|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|WD=Q772647|lt=Mizubashō|1=Lysichiton camtschatcensis}}]] Tsuruno-yu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|en|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|WD=Q8514544|lt=Satake Yoshitaka|1=Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩|1=Kubota Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067720}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|Avalanche|en|Avalanche|WD=Q7935|lt=avalanche|1=Avalanche}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|1=Q11054479|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television|en|Nippon Television|WD=Q864910|lt=Nippon TV|1=Nippon Television}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshirō Yanagiba|en|Toshirō Yanagiba|WD=Q1135347|lt=Toshio Yanagiba|1=Toshirō Yanagiba}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|WD=Q725522|lt=Foot Bath|1=Ashiyu}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} and then the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|1=Q11446830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] {{wikidata link|Q11675866}} [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen (Akita)]][[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Tsuruno-yu Onsen |image = Tsuruno-yu Onsen photographed in autumn 2022 (6).jpg |Comment = Tsuruno-yu Onsen Main Lodge ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}){{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |Location = {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} Tazawako, Tazawa, Yunodai 2-ban Saki Sakitatsuzawa, National Forest Seihonai, Business District 50, Forest Unit 8, Subunit |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|18.4|N|140|46|48.4|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鶴の湯温泉}} |Access = {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}}{{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} via {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}}’s {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}} for about 40 minutes; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Then, 1.5 km on foot.
By car: From {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}} for about 60 minutes. |Spring Quality = Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring, etc.
''For details, see the {{ill|Water Quality|ja|泉質|1=Hot Spring Types|lt=Water Quality|lt_ja=泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water Quality|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=}} section.'' |Spring Temperature = |Flow Rate = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bathing Guests = |Statistical Year = |External Link = http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ |Special Notes = }} '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|WD=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}} {{ill|Semboku, Akita|en|Semboku, Akita|WD=Q846877|lt=Semboku City|1=Semboku, Akita}} within {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. There is a {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan|1=Ryokan}} named "Tsuruno-yu Onsen" and its annex "Yama no Yado". == Water Quality == White Hot Water (Shiro-yu), Black Hot Water (Kuro-yu), Middle Hot Water (Naka-no-yu), and Waterfall Hot Water (Taki-no-yu) are the four types of springs available, with the following qualities: * Shiro-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium–Calcium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide type) * Kuro-yu: Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Salt Spring * Naka-no-yu: Containing Baking Soda, Saline Hydrogen Sulfide Spring * Taki-no-yu: Sulfur-containing, Sodium Chloride, Bicarbonate Spring == Onsen Town == [[File:Tsurunoyu Yama-no-yado (3).jpg|thumb|250px|Tsuruno-yu Onsen – Yama no Yado]] Off the prefectural road, along a mountain path, nestled within a primeval beech forest, there is a single inn named {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen|qq=}} which is also a member of {{ill|Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|ja|日本秘湯を守る会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Society for the Protection of Japan's Secret Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The proprietor, Kazushi Sato, serves as the vice president of the society. The building is famous for its thatched-roof {{ill|Honjin|en|Honjin|WD=Q994817|lt=Main Lodge|1=Honjin}}, and the entire complex, including the other structures, maintains a rustic aesthetic. The mixed-gender {{ill|Open-Air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}, where Shiro-yu gushes from the ground, is emblematic of Tsuruno-yu Onsen's image. This open-air bath is frequently featured when introducing {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}, and is widely recognized as representative of the image of a {{ill|Secret Hot Spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}. Along the {{ill|Forest track|en|Forest track|WD=Q3444700|lt=Forest Road|1=Forest track}} in front of the ryokan, there is the annex "Yama no Yado", supplied with water from the main building and equipped with a private open-air bath. Guests at Yama no Yado can also enjoy the hot spring facilities of the main building. It is a quiet inn distinct from the main building, which is frequented by day-trip bathers. == History == [[File:Mizubasyou002.jpg|180px|right|thumb|"Tsuruno-yu Onsen" within the premises – {{ill|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|en|Lysichiton camtschatcensis|WD=Q772647|lt=Mizubashō|1=Lysichiton camtschatcensis}}]] Tsuruno-yu Onsen is said to be the oldest hot spring in {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. Its discovery is attributed to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. At its inception, it was called "Tazawa no Yu". It is recorded that {{ill|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|en|Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)|WD=Q8514544|lt=Satake Yoshitaka|1=Satake Yoshitaka (1609–1672)}} of the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩|1=Kubota Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067720}} visited for {{ill|therapeutic bathing|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}, and the existence of the main lodge is a remnant of that era. However, it is said that guards were lodged at this main lodge. This main lodge was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} in 2010. In 1708 (the 5th year of the {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei era|1=Hōei}}), a matagi (mountain hunter) named Kansuke discovered a crane healing its wound. In commemoration of this event, the hot spring was renamed from "Tazawa no Yu" to "Tsurunoyu". The single inn was established afterward and operated as a {{ill|therapeutic bathing facility|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic bathing facility|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}} during the off‐season. On October 19, 1967 (Showa 42), it was designated as a {{ill|National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} as part of {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}}. In the 1980s, it gained significant attention amid the secret hot spring boom. In February 2006 (Heisei 18), an {{ill|Avalanche|en|Avalanche|WD=Q7935|lt=avalanche|1=Avalanche}} occurred due to the heavy snowfall of the {{ill|Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja|平成18th year 豪雪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei 18 Heavy Snow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, resulting in the death of one employee and injuries to 16 guests and others{{ill|Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja|秋田県仙北市田沢湖で雪崩相次ぐ|lt=Wiki News|lt_ja=ウィキニュース|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wiki News: Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Consequently, during the winter season, the main lodge was temporarily closed and only the annex operated, but business resumed on {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} of the same year. On March 5, 2011 (Heisei 23), the television program {{ill|Arashi ni Shiyagare|ja|嵐にしやがれ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashi ni Shiyagare|1=Q11054479|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11054479}} (aired on {{ill|Nippon Television|en|Nippon Television|WD=Q864910|lt=Nippon TV|1=Nippon Television}}) featured guest {{ill|Toshirō Yanagiba|en|Toshirō Yanagiba|WD=Q1135347|lt=Toshio Yanagiba|1=Toshirō Yanagiba}}, a native and resident of Akita Prefecture. A studio set replicating the onsen was constructed, and the hot spring water was utilized as a {{ill|Ashiyu|en|Ashiyu|WD=Q725522|lt=Foot Bath|1=Ashiyu}}, which is not normally in operation. == Access == * Public Transport: Approximately 40 minutes via {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|en|Akita Shinkansen|WD=Q220696|lt=Akita Shinkansen|1=Akita Shinkansen}} from {{ill|Tazawako Station|en|Tazawako Station|WD=Q692529|lt=Tazawako Station|1=Tazawako Station}} and then the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|WD=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} of {{ill|Ugokōtsū|ja|羽後交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugokōtsū|1=Ugo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610147}} bound for {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūtō Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 乳頭温泉郷|1=Nyūtō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370596}}; alight at {{ill|Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja|鶴の湯温泉入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruno-yu Onsen Entrance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, then a 1.5 km walk. :: Alternatively, alight at the "Arupa Komakusa" bus stop on the same route, followed by a shuttle service (reservation required). * By Car: Approximately 60 minutes from {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Morioka Interchange|ja|盛岡インターチェンジ|lt=Morioka IC|lt_ja=盛岡IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morioka Interchange|1=Morioka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581337}}, via {{ill|Japan National Route 46|en|Japan National Route 46|WD=Q6157611|lt=National Route 46|1=Japan National Route 46}} and {{ill|Japan National Route 341|en|Japan National Route 341|WD=Q6157289|lt=National Route 341|1=Japan National Route 341}}, then via {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127 Komagadake Line|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|ja|岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|lt_ja=秋田県道194号西山生保内線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194 Nishiyama-Seihonai Line|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. Cars may proceed up to the front of the ryokan. == Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|en|Nyūtō Onsen|WD=Q11370596|lt=Nyūtō Onsenkyo|1=Nyūtō Onsen}} ** {{ill|Kuroyu Onsen|ja|黒湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} ** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}} ** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}} ** {{ill|Ogama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogama Onsen|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}} ** {{ill|Myounoyu Onsen|ja|妙乃湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myounoyu Onsen|1=Q11446830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446830}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Wikinews|Snow Avalanches in Tazawako, Semboku, Akita}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurunoyu.com/ Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Ryokan homepage) * [https://blog.goo.ne.jp/tsurunoyu1 Tsuruno-yu Onsen] (Blog) * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/tsurunoyu.html Tsurunoyu] – Nyūtō Onsen Association {{Onsen}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)]] [[ko:쓰루노유 온천]] {{wikidata link|Q11675866}} [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
